[[John Monie]] (Sep 99 - Nov 00) | captain = | stadium = [[The Stoop]] | avg_attendance = 2,805 | high_attendance = 8,233 | top_tries = [[Greg Fleming (footballer)|Greg Fleming]] - 26 | top_goals = [[Brett Warton]] - 59 | top_points = Brett Warton - 130 | pattern_la1 = _darkyellowthickborder | pattern_b1 = _yellowblackyellowhorizontal | pattern_ra1 = _darkyellowthickborder | pattern_sh1 = _redsides | pattern_so1 = _whitetop | leftarm1 = ff0000 | body1 = ff0000 | rightarm1 = ff0000 | shorts1 = | socks1 = ff0000 | pattern_la2 = _redyellow | pattern_b2 = _whiteredwhite_hoops | pattern_ra2 = _redyellow | pattern_sh2 = _redsides | pattern_so2 = _whitetop | leftarm2 = 002387 | body2 = 002387 | rightarm2 = 002387 | shorts2 = | socks2 = 002366 | prev_season = [[1998 London Broncos season|1998]] | next_season = [[2000 London Broncos season|2000]] | list = yes | list_link = List of London Broncos seasons }} The '''1999 London Broncos season''' was the twentieth in the club's history and their fourth season in the [[Super League]]. The Broncos had three coaches over the course of the season, competing in [[Super League IV]] and finishing in 8th place. The club also made their maiden appearance in the final of the [[1999 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]]. ==Super League IV table== {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |- ! style="width:20px;" ==1999 Challenge Cup== The [[London Broncos]] progressed to the Final of the Cup, before losing to the [[Leeds Rhinos]] by 52–16 at the [[Old Wembley Stadium]] in [[London]].{{cite web|title=Sport: Rugby League Rhino charge overpowers Broncos|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/1/hi/sport/rugby_league/333483.stm|work=BBC Sport|access-date=26 January 2014|date=1 May 1999}}{{cite
